prompt:
The hot `CacheChain` traversal in `src/cache_chain.cpp` performs every ID lookup through a node-based `std::unordered_map`. The protected `layout_benchmark` uses deterministic instrumentation, not wall-clock timing: it exposes one index pointer chase on every step and node-per-ID allocation during dense index construction. Improve the private index layout so the dense-ID benchmark uses at most 64 dynamic allocations between handing its populated node vector to `CacheChain` and completing construction, then traverses with zero index pointer chases. `index_reads` must remain an honest count of lookup attempts (one per completed benchmark step), and `index_pointer_chases` must continue to count node-based index traversals rather than merely being suppressed.

Keep the public types and signatures unchanged and retain correct behavior for sparse IDs across the full `std::uint32_t` range without allocating storage proportional to the largest sparse ID. Do not change CLC1 serialization bytes, stored iteration order, traversal step order, or the bit-for-bit computed sum. A hybrid representation is allowed, and profiling counters may differ between dense and sparse representations as long as they truthfully describe the implementation.

Run `make test` from the repository root. Do not modify `.gitignore`, `Makefile`, `CMakeLists.txt`, `include/cache_chain.hpp`, or either file under `tests/`.

full_distill.primary_skill: replace a node-based hot-path lookup with a cache-local dense index while retaining a bounded sparse-ID fallback and exact observable behavior
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero because dense index construction allocates one hash node per ID and traversal reports one node-based index pointer chase for every benchmark step
full_distill.success_contract: dense-ID index construction uses at most 64 dynamic allocations and traversal reports zero index pointer chases with one honest index read per step, while sparse 32-bit IDs, iteration order, serialization bytes, traversal order, and bit-for-bit sums remain correct
adversarial_factors: deterministic allocation and operation counters reject wall-clock-dependent performance claims, counter suppression, and a node-based dense index
a sparse fixture containing UINT32_MAX rejects a single flat allocation sized by the largest ID
prefix-by-prefix traversal sums pin the exact next-ID walk rather than checking only one aggregate cycle sum
byte-exact CLC1 fixtures and iteration assertions prevent the private layout change from leaking into the storage or public ordering contracts
